Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Users Request Input An event log for an Insurance Claims Processing process with 5 cases 2001 2005 Attributes per event Case ID Timestamp Activity Resource Region Complexity Process Steps Submit Claim to Evaluate Claim to Request Additional Documents to Approve Claim to Pay Claim to Close Claim Task Identify cases with significantly longer durations Analyze attributes Resource Region Complexity to find root causes of long durations Propose explanations and mitigation suggestions

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Calculate Case Durations Lead Times I need to compute the total duration for each case from the first event Submit Claim to the last event Close Claim Case 2001 1h 30m Case 2002 25h 55m Case 2003 48h 20m Case 2004 1h 25m Case 2005 77h 05m Clearly Cases 2003 and 2005 take significantly longer Case 2002 is moderately long Cases 2001 and 2004 are very fast

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze Attributes for Long Cases vs Short Cases 2001 Region A Complexity Low Duration fast No doc requests 2002 Region B Complexity Medium Duration about 26h 1 doc request 2003 Region A Complexity High Duration about 48h 2 doc requests 2004 Region B Complexity Low Duration fast No doc requests 2005 Region B Complexity High Duration about 77h 3 doc requests

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Observations Complexity Low complexity cases 2001 2004 are fast Medium 2002 is about 26h High 2003 2005 are about 48h and 77h Strong correlation between Complexity and Duration Request Additional Documents Low complexity equals 0 requests Medium equals 1 request High equals 2 or 3 requests Each request seems to add significant waiting time

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Region Both Region A and B have fast cases 2001 A 2004 B and slow cases 2003 A 2005 B Region does not seem to be a primary driver

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Resource Lisa handles both fast 2004 and slow 2005 cases Mike handles both fast 2001 and slow 2003 cases Manager Bill only appears on High complexity cases but that is likely because high complexity requires a different senior manager not necessarily the cause of delay The delays happen before approval during the Request Additional Documents loops

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The bottleneck seems to be the iterative Request Additional Documents step which correlates directly with Complexity

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Primary Root Cause High Complexity claims trigger multiple Request Additional Documents cycles Each cycle introduces significant waiting time likely external customer dependent drastically increasing lead time Secondary Contributing Factors The process lacks a standardized checklist or upfront validation for high complexity claims leading to iterative piecemeal document requests Resource allocation suggests either heavy workload lack of training on complex claims or systemic process inefficiency rather than individual performance issues Region does not show a clear pattern so it is likely not a root cause

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Explanation High complexity claims inherently require more documentation The current process handles this reactively evaluate find missing doc request wait evaluate again find another missing doc request again This iterative loop causes exponential delays due to customer response times and internal re queuing

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Time gaps The biggest gaps are between Request Additional Documents and the next activity For 2005 Apr 1 1130 to Apr 2 1700 29h 30m Apr 2 1700 to Apr 3 1500 22h Apr 3 1500 to Apr 4 1000 19h These are clearly waiting periods for customer responses or internal rework Resources No single resource is consistently slow across complexity levels Region A and B both have fast and slow cases Manager Bill handles High cases but the delay happens before approval

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Mitigation Upfront Document Checklist Validation Implement complexity based submission checklists For High complexity require specific documents at submission Use automated validation to flag missing items immediately Batch Document Requests Instead of multiple sequential requests train adjusters to identify all missing documents in the first evaluation and send a single comprehensive request Specialized team for High Complexity Assign dedicated senior adjusters or a complex claims team with stricter service targets and proactive follow up Customer Portal Enable digital upload with real time validation Process Monitoring Set up alerts for cases with more than 1 document request to trigger intervention

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Check 2002 duration 25h 55m correct Check 2003 duration 48h 20m correct Check 2005 duration 77h 05m correct Attribute analysis matches data Recommendations are practical and process improvement focused All prompt requirements met
